description_long: This is a small, vintage-style biplane with a predominantly pink fuselage and wings. The aircraft features two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, connected by struts, and a single propeller mounted at the nose. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail wheel, characteristic of a taildragger configuration. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windshield visible. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a conventional tail design. The overall appearance suggests a lightweight, classic aircraft suitable for recreational or display purposes in an urban park or museum setting.
description: A small, pink vintage biplane with stacked wings, open cockpit, and taildragger landing gear. Features a single propeller and classic tail design.
description_view_0: The airplane is seen from the front, highlighting the propeller, open cockpit, and the stacked biplane wings.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the full length of the fuselage, the two main landing wheels, and the tail wheel, as well as the open cockpit position.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the tail section, including the single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, with the wings extending outward.
description_view_3: From the opposite side, the biplane's wingspan and landing gear are visible, along with the propeller and cockpit placement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward on the fuselage.
